name: azure-keyvault-py
description: Azure Key Vault SDK for Python. Use for secrets, keys, and certificates management with secure storage.
capabilities: Create, retrieve, and manage secrets in Azure Key Vault with version support
Generate and manage cryptographic keys (RSA, EC) for encryption operations
Perform encrypt, decrypt, sign, and verify operations using CryptographyClient
Create, import, and manage SSL/TLS certificates with self-signed options
Handle soft-delete and purge operations for recovery scenarios
Use async clients for high-throughput scenarios with proper error handling
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains documentation-only content for Azure Key Vault SDK for Python. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of executable code, resulting in a risk score of 0/100. The SKILL.md file contains standard code examples for Azure SDK usage with no security concerns. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Securely store and access sensitive data using Azure Key Vault. This skill enables developers to implement enterprise-grade secret management with minimal setup.
userTitle: Manage Azure secrets, keys, and certificates with Python SDK
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and Key Vault resource to be pre-configured
Network connectivity to Azure Key Vault endpoint is required for all operations
RBAC permissions must be configured separately for each Key Vault
Does not support on-premises or self-hosted Key Vault alternatives

useCases.title: Database Credential Management
useCases.description: Store and rotate database passwords securely without hardcoding in application code. Applications retrieve credentials at runtime using managed identity.

useCases.title: API Key and Token Storage
useCases.description: Centralize storage for third-party API keys, tokens, and connection strings with automatic rotation and audit logging.

useCases.targetUser: Security architects implementing data protection strategies
useCases.title: Document Encryption at Rest
useCases.description: Use Key Vault cryptographic keys to encrypt sensitive documents and data before storage, ensuring only authorized services can decrypt.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Secret Retrieval
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me retrieve a secret named 'database-connection-string' from my Azure Key Vault using Python. My vault URL is https://myvault.vault.azure.net/
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner: First-time Key Vault user needs to access a stored secret

promptTemplates.title: Create and Version a Secret
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a new secret with a value and then retrieve a specific previous version using the Azure Key Vault Python SDK.
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate: Implementing secret versioning for rollback capability

promptTemplates.title: Encrypt and Decrypt Data
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to encrypt a file using RSA encryption with a key stored in Azure Key Vault. Write Python code using CryptographyClient to encrypt the file and then decrypt it later.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced: Implementing client-side encryption with Key Vault keys

promptTemplates.title: Async Secret Management with Error Handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an async Python function that retrieves multiple secrets from Key Vault, handles ResourceNotFoundError and HttpResponseError appropriately, and returns a dictionary of secret names to values.
promptTemplates.scenario: Expert: Production-ready async implementation with comprehensive error handling

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for authentication which supports multiple credential types including managed identity, environment credentials, and Azure CLI
Enable soft-delete on Key Vault to recover accidentally deleted secrets, keys, and certificates within the retention period
Implement secret rotation by creating new versions instead of overwriting, and update application references to use the latest version
antiPatterns: Never hardcode Key Vault URLs or secrets directly in application code - use environment variables or configuration
Avoid storing large amounts of data in secrets - Key Vault is designed for small sensitive values like passwords and connection strings
Do not skip error handling for 403 Forbidden responses - this usually indicates missing RBAC permissions that need to be addressed

faq.question: What authentication methods does DefaultAzureCredential support?
faq.answer: DefaultAzureCredential automatically tries multiple methods: managed identity (in Azure), environment credentials (AZURE_CLIENT_ID, etc.), Azure CLI login, Visual Studio credentials, and more. It uses the first successful method.

faq.question: How do I enable soft-delete on my Key Vault?
faq.answer: Soft-delete is enabled by default on all new Key Vaults. For existing vaults, check the Azure portal under Access Policies. Once enabled, deleted items are retained for 7-90 days and can be recovered.

faq.question: What is the difference between access policies and RBAC for Key Vault?
faq.answer: RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) provides more granular permissions and is recommended for new implementations. Access policies are simpler but less flexible. You can use either model, but not both simultaneously on the same vault.

faq.question: Can I use Azure Key Vault from on-premises applications?
faq.answer: Yes, on-premises applications can access Key Vault over HTTPS. You need to authenticate using Azure AD credentials, service principals, or managed identity if using Azure Arc-enabled servers.

faq.question: How do I rotate secrets automatically in Key Vault?
faq.answer: Key Vault supports automatic rotation for secrets with configured policies. You can set rotation triggers based on time intervals. Alternatively, implement manual rotation by creating new versions using set_secret() before the old version expires.

faq.question: What happens to my secrets if I delete the Key Vault?
faq.answer: If soft-delete is enabled (default), deleted vaults and their contents are retained for 7-90 days and can be recovered. After the retention period expires or if purged, recovery is not possible. Always enable soft-delete in production.
